markdown:
## **Why You Need** **to Make the Global Issue Obvious**


1. **Okay, here’s the deal: **if the examiner can’t tell what your global issue is by the first 30 seconds, you’ve already made their job harder, and yours worse. 
2. The** IO isn’t just “talk about a theme”; it’s _explore a global issue and show how the author represents it_.** 

![Whatever you do, make sure the global issue is obvious!](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a1021c81a4d82d17e54c46da3df88a9ca9097b23-600x450.jpg)

## Step-by-Step: How to Make the Global Issue Clear


### **Step 1: Say the global issue clearly at the start.**


1. **Don’t dance around it.** Say something like:
  1. “This extract explores the global issue of gender inequality in patriarchal societies.”

### **Step 2: Keep linking back to it.**


1. When you **analyze a quote or technique, connect it back to the global issue.**

<callout type="example">“This use of religious imagery highlights the control placed on women, reinforcing the global issue of institutionalized gender oppression.”</callout>

### **Step 3: Use confident linking phrases.**


1. Use phrases like:
  1. “This reflects the global issue…”
  2. “This moment draws attention to…”
  3. “The writer critiques…”
2. These **phrases help keep your argument focused and sharp.**

### **Step 4: Evaluate how well the writer explores the issue.**


1. Don’t just describe what the author does
2. **Explain why it works or doesn’t work in making the issue powerful or relatable.**

<callout type="example">“By using irony here, the author forces the audience to confront just how normalised this injustice has become.”</callout>

### **Step 5: Bring it back in the conclusion.**


1. Finish by** showing how the extract’s representation of the global issue matters to the reader, to society, or to the world.**

<callout type="example">“In the end, the text uses a single character’s struggle to shine a light on the wider impact of gender-based discrimination, making the global issue feel personal and urgent.”</callout>

<callout type="note">Bottom line: **if you don’t clearly and repeatedly show how your analysis connects to the global issue, it won’t stick**, and neither will your marks.</callout>
